homepage.heroSubline: Built to last through Colorado's toughest weather. Serving Denver metro homes and businesses since 2012.
aboutText: Founded in 2012, Legacy Concrete started with one truck and a commitment to quality workmanship. Today, we're Denver's trusted name for durable concrete solutions that withstand Colorado's extreme climate.
We approach every project with precision and pride, from historic Capitol Hill repairs to modern Highlands Ranch installations. Our crews know local building codes and weather challenges inside out.
When we pour concrete in Denver, we're building more than slabs - we're strengthening our community's foundations for decades to come.

valueProps.title: Weather-Proven
valueProps.description: Our mixes are formulated for Denver's freeze-thaw cycles and hail impacts.

valueProps.title: Local Knowledge
valueProps.description: We understand Denver's historic districts and modern construction requirements.

valueProps.title: No Shortcuts
valueProps.description: Proper site prep and curing techniques ensure long-term durability.

valueProps.title: Transparent Pricing
valueProps.description: Upfront quotes with no surprise charges - Denver deserves honest contractors.

homepage.ctaSubline: Call 555-con-rq19 for a free Denver-area estimate today.

services.name: Driveway Installation
services.shortDescription: Heavy-duty driveways built for Denver's climate challenges.
services.description:
Colorado's freeze-thaw cycles destroy inferior concrete within years. Our driveway installations use 4000 PSI mix with fiber reinforcement and proper expansion joints to prevent cracking.

We excavate 12 inches below grade in Denver's clay soils, then install proper gravel base and vapor barriers. Every pour includes control joints spaced for our climate. Finish options include brushed, exposed aggregate, or stamped patterns that complement your home's architecture.

From historic Park Hill bungalows to modern Highlands Ranch estates, we've installed driveways that stand the test of time.
process: Step 1: Site evaluation including soil testing and drainage assessment
Step 2: Excavation and compacted gravel base installation
Step 3: Form building with proper expansion joint planning
Step 4: Pouring and finishing with climate-appropriate techniques
benefits: Thicker slabs resist cracking from freeze-thaw cycles
Proper base prevents settling in Denver's expansive soils
Reinforced edges withstand snowplow impacts
Custom finishes to match your home's style

faq.question: How thick should a Denver driveway be?
faq.answer: We pour minimum 5-inch slabs with thickened edges - thinner concrete fails quickly here. Historic districts may require special approvals we handle.

faq.question: When's the best time to install?
faq.answer: Spring through early fall allows proper curing. We monitor weather closely - no pours if temps drop below 40°F.

faq.question: Do you repair existing driveways?
faq.answer: Yes, but Denver's climate often makes full replacement more cost-effective long-term. We'll give you straight advice.

services.name: Foundation Repair
services.shortDescription: Solutions for Denver's shifting soils and freeze damage.
services.description:
Denver's clay soils expand and contract dramatically with moisture changes, while freeze-thaw cycles accelerate foundation problems. We specialize in diagnosing and repairing these Colorado-specific issues.

Our team assesses cracks, uneven floors, and sticking doors to determine the root cause. Solutions range from epoxy injections for minor cracks to helical piers for serious settling. We coordinate with structural engineers when needed, especially in historic neighborhoods like LoDo.

For older homes, we match original concrete composition and finishing techniques. Modern builds get updated reinforcement methods. Every repair includes drainage improvements to prevent recurrence.
process: Step 1: Comprehensive inspection including moisture testing
Step 2: Laser level measurements to identify settlement patterns
Step 3: Custom repair plan with material options
Step 4: Precision execution with minimal disruption
benefits: Localized repairs preserve structural integrity
Solutions tailored to Denver's soil conditions
Prevent further damage to your home
Increased property value with proper documentation

faq.question: How do I know if my foundation needs repair?
faq.answer: Horizontal cracks wider than 1/4 inch, doors that won't close, or visible gaps between walls and floors all indicate problems. We offer free evaluations.

faq.question: Will repairs prevent future issues?
faq.answer: Our solutions address both symptoms and causes, including proper grading and drainage - critical in Denver's climate.

faq.question: How long does foundation repair take?
faq.answer: Most projects complete in 2-5 days. Historic homes may require longer for careful matching of materials.

services.name: Patio Installation
services.shortDescription: Outdoor living spaces designed for Denver's seasons.
services.description:
From RiNo backyard retreats to Wash Park garden patios, we build outdoor spaces that handle Colorado's extremes. Our designs consider sun exposure, snow load, and hail resistance.

We offer poured concrete patios with options from simple brushed finishes to decorative stamping that mimics natural stone. For sloping yards common in Lakewood and Arvada, we incorporate retaining walls and proper drainage.

Every installation includes sub-surface preparation critical in our climate - compacted base material, proper slope for runoff, and control joints placed to prevent unsightly cracking. Optional radiant heat systems extend usability into cooler months.
process: Step 1: Consultation to discuss usage and design preferences
Step 2: Site preparation including grading analysis
Step 3: Form building with precise elevation planning
Step 4: Pouring and finishing with selected textures
benefits: Durable surfaces that withstand hail and freeze cycles
Custom shapes to fit your outdoor space perfectly
Low-maintenance compared to wood or pavers
Increased home value with professional installation

faq.question: What's better for Denver - concrete or pavers?
faq.answer: Poured concrete requires less maintenance and handles freeze-thaw better when properly installed. We can discuss both options.

faq.question: Can you match my existing concrete?
faq.answer: We'll take samples to match color and texture for seamless additions to older patios.

faq.question: How soon can I use my new patio?
faq.answer: Wait 7 days for foot traffic, 28 days for heavy furniture. We'll provide exact timelines based on weather.

services.name: Sidewalk Replacement
services.shortDescription: ADA-compliant walkways that endure Colorado winters.
services.description:
Denver's sidewalk repair ordinances demand durable solutions. We replace damaged walkways with mixes designed to resist salt damage and freeze-thaw stress.

Our crews work efficiently to minimize neighborhood disruption, carefully removing old concrete without damaging surrounding landscaping. New pours meet strict ADA slope requirements while blending with your home's aesthetic.

We coordinate with city inspectors in historic districts like Capitol Hill, ensuring compliance with preservation guidelines. For commercial properties, we schedule work during low-traffic periods.
process: Step 1: Inspection of existing walkway and subbase
Step 2: Removal with minimal vibration to nearby structures
Step 3: Form setting with precise elevation control
Step 4: Pouring and finishing to municipal specifications
benefits: Eliminate trip hazards and liability concerns
Proper slope prevents ice buildup in winter
Salt-resistant finishes maintain appearance
Fast completion with neighborhood consideration

faq.question: Who handles city permits?
faq.answer: We manage all Denver permitting including historic district approvals when needed.

faq.question: Can tree roots damage new sidewalks?
faq.answer: We install root barriers where needed and use reinforcement to prevent future damage.

faq.question: How long until I can walk on it?
faq.answer: 48 hours for foot traffic, full cure in 7 days. We'll mark protected areas clearly.

services.name: Concrete Floors
services.shortDescription: Industrial-grade finishes for Denver homes and businesses.
services.description:
From RiNo loft conversions to Arvada basement remodels, polished concrete floors offer durability perfect for Colorado living. Our process transforms slabs into showpiece surfaces.

We grind existing concrete to expose aggregate, then apply penetrating sealers that resist stains and moisture - critical in Denver's dry climate. For new construction, we pour slabs with integrated color and proper vapor barriers.

Commercial clients appreciate our non-slip finishes that handle heavy foot traffic. Residential customers love the radiant heat compatibility and easy maintenance compared to other flooring options.
process: Step 1: Slab evaluation for cracks and moisture content
Step 2: Mechanical grinding to desired aggregate exposure
Step 3: Application of densifiers and sealers
Step 4: Final polishing for specified sheen level
benefits: No grout lines or seams to collect dirt
Naturally cool in summer, perfect for radiant heat
Withstands heavy furniture and pet traffic
Modern aesthetic that complements any decor

faq.question: Can you polish my existing basement floor?
faq.answer: Most slabs can be transformed unless severely damaged. We'll assess structural integrity first.

faq.question: How slippery are polished floors?
faq.answer: Our commercial-grade finishes include anti-slip additives without sacrificing appearance.

faq.question: Do polished floors work with radiant heat?
faq.answer: Absolutely - concrete's thermal mass makes it ideal for radiant systems common in Denver homes.

services.name: Retaining Walls
services.shortDescription: Structural solutions for Denver's sloped properties.
services.description:
Colorado's varied terrain demands retaining walls that do more than look good - they must hold back tons of earth through seasonal shifts. Our poured concrete walls outperform block systems in durability.

We engineer walls specifically for Denver's soil conditions, incorporating proper drainage and reinforcement. From tiered garden walls in Wash Park to erosion control in Littleton hillsides, each project begins with thorough load analysis.

Finishes range from natural stone veneers to smooth modern surfaces. We coordinate with landscapers when desired, ensuring seamless integration with your outdoor space.
process: Step 1: Site survey and soil testing
Step 2: Engineering analysis for load requirements
Step 3: Form building with rebar placement
Step 4: Pouring and finishing with selected texture
benefits: No shifting or bulging like block walls
Engineered for Denver's freeze-thaw cycles
Custom heights and curves to fit your space
Increased usable yard area

faq.question: How tall can concrete retaining walls be?
faq.answer: We engineer walls up to 6 feet tall without additional permits. Taller walls require structural plans we can provide.

faq.question: Do walls need maintenance?
faq.answer: Properly built concrete walls require virtually no upkeep - we design them to last decades.

faq.question: Can you match my existing hardscape?
faq.answer: We'll sample nearby concrete to blend new walls seamlessly with your property.

services.name: Garage Floors
services.shortDescription: Heavy-duty surfaces for Denver's garages and workshops.
services.description:
Colorado vehicles bring in salt, oil, and chemicals that destroy standard garage floors. Our epoxy and polyurethane coatings create impervious surfaces that shrug off these challenges.

We start by diamond grinding existing concrete to ensure perfect adhesion. Then apply industrial-grade coatings resistant to hot tires, chemical spills, and impact. Optional flake finishes provide traction during snowy months.

For new construction, we pour slabs with integrated vapor barriers and steel fibers to prevent cracking. Many Highlands Ranch homeowners choose our radiant heat compatible finishes for year-round workshop comfort.
process: Step 1: Surface preparation including crack repair
Step 2: Mechanical profiling for coating adhesion
Step 3: Primer and base coat application
Step 4: Topcoat with selected finish and additives
benefits: No more dusting or spalling concrete
Chemical-resistant surfaces easy to clean
Custom colors and decorative options
Increased home value with finished garage space

faq.question: How long does the coating last?
faq.answer: Our commercial-grade systems typically last 10+ years with proper maintenance - far longer than DIY kits.

faq.question: Can you coat cracked floors?
faq.answer: We first repair cracks with flexible epoxy systems designed for movement in Denver's climate.

faq.question: How soon can I park on it?
faq.answer: 72 hours for light vehicles, full cure in 7 days. We provide exact timelines based on temperature.

reviews.name: Mark T.
reviews.text: Legacy replaced our cracked 1980s driveway in Park Hill with a reinforced concrete slab that's survived two Denver winters without a single crack. The crew worked around our mature trees carefully and cleaned up perfectly.

reviews.name: Sarah K.
reviews.text: After our Arvada ranch home started showing foundation cracks, Legacy diagnosed the issue as improper original drainage. Their helical pier solution stabilized the house and the new grading has kept everything dry since.

reviews.name: James and Lori W.
reviews.text: Our new stamped concrete patio in Wash Park looks like flagstone but handles Denver hail without chipping. The crew incorporated subtle drainage slopes we never would have thought about - no puddles after heavy rains.

reviews.name: Denver Ortho Clinic
reviews.text: Legacy transformed our RiNo medical office's worn floors into polished masterpieces. The non-slip finish meets ADA requirements while looking modern and professional. Zero downtime during the process.

reviews.name: Roberta S.
reviews.text: Fast, compliant sidewalk replacement in Capitol Hill historic district. Legacy handled all the city permits and matched the original 1920s finish perfectly. Neighbors have asked for their contact info.

reviews.name: Mike D.
reviews.text: The 4-foot concrete retaining wall in our Littleton backyard stopped our erosion problems completely. It's been through two springs now with zero movement or cracking - just solid performance.

service-areas.title: Denver Metro Concrete Services | Legacy Concrete
service-areas.description: Proudly serving Denver, Aurora, Lakewood, Arvada, Littleton and Highlands Ranch with durable concrete solutions.

serviceAreas.content: Legacy Concrete proudly serves Aurora, Colorado, a bustling city with a population of approximately 395,000 residents. Known for its 1970s-80s suburban developments, Aurora's homes often require durable concrete solutions to withstand the region's cold winters and heavy snowfall. Our team specializes in repairing and reinforcing driveways, patios, and foundations to ensure longevity in Aurora's challenging climate. Whether you need a new concrete slab or repairs to existing structures, Legacy Concrete delivers top-quality work tailored to Aurora's unique needs.
serviceAreas.localContext: Aurora features sprawling suburban neighborhoods built primarily in the 1970s and 1980s, with a climate that demands weather-resistant concrete solutions.

serviceAreas.content: Lakewood, Colorado, is home to over 157,000 residents and is characterized by its charming 1960s-70s split-level homes. Legacy Concrete provides expert services to address the wear and tear these older properties face, especially in Lakewood's cold winters with heavy snow. From cracked sidewalks to aging foundations, our team ensures your concrete structures remain sturdy and safe. We also offer decorative concrete options to enhance Lakewood's mid-century aesthetic while maintaining durability.
serviceAreas.localContext: Lakewood's architectural landscape is dominated by split-level homes from the 1960s and 1970s, requiring specialized concrete maintenance.

serviceAreas.content: Arvada, Colorado, with its population of around 124,000, is known for its classic 1960s-70s ranch-style homes. Legacy Concrete understands the unique challenges these properties face, particularly in Arvada's cold, snowy winters. Our services include foundation repairs, driveway replacements, and custom concrete work to preserve the integrity of Arvada's homes. Trust us to deliver durable, weather-resistant solutions that blend seamlessly with the city's timeless architectural style.
serviceAreas.localContext: Arvada's ranch-style homes from the 1960s and 1970s require concrete solutions that balance functionality with the area's rustic charm.

serviceAreas.content: Littleton, Colorado, is a picturesque city of about 48,000 residents, famous for its historic downtown bungalows. Legacy Concrete specializes in preserving and enhancing these charming properties with high-quality concrete work. Littleton's cold winters and heavy snowfall demand durable solutions for driveways, walkways, and foundations. Whether you're restoring a historic home or adding modern concrete features, our team ensures your project meets Littleton's high standards.
serviceAreas.localContext: Littleton's historic bungalows and downtown area require concrete solutions that respect the city's heritage while ensuring durability.

serviceAreas.content: Highlands Ranch, Colorado, is a master-planned community with a population of approximately 105,000. Known for its 1990s-2000s homes, this area benefits from Legacy Concrete's expertise in modern concrete applications. From sleek stamped patios to sturdy driveways, we cater to Highlands Ranch's contemporary aesthetic while addressing the challenges of cold winters and heavy snow. Our team ensures your concrete projects enhance both functionality and curb appeal.
serviceAreas.localContext: Highlands Ranch's planned community features newer homes that benefit from modern concrete techniques and designs.

The city's cold winters and heavy snowfall require durable concrete work, and our team excels in delivering weather-resistant driveways, sidewalks, and foundations. Whether you're in a historic neighborhood or a modern high-rise, Legacy Concrete has you covered.
serviceAreas.localContext: Denver's mix of historic and modern architecture demands versatile concrete solutions for its harsh winter climate.
